plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk. Trim QQQI and QQQ given their downtrends. Add to CRWD, a strong uptrending name. Build a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a ~1.5%/month outlook with significantly reduced drawdown risk. It prioritizes capital preservation by cutting all leveraged ETFs and building a substantial cash buffer.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits. Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove flagged leveraged ETFs. Reduce QQQI and QQQ exposure due to their downtrends. Add to CRWD, a strong performing name. Initiate a new position in NVDA from the scanner's top setups.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month growth with a focus on risk management. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index exposure with quality tech names.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits. Reduce SOXL to 5% to maintain some leveraged exposure. Sell SPAL to exit the flagged leveraged ETF. Trim QQQI and QQQ to reduce exposure to downtrends. Increase CRWD to 20% to press a strong performer. Buy NVDA at 10% as a high-scoring scanner setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for ~4%/month growth by leaning into high-scoring setups and maintaining some leveraged exposure. This approach significantly raises both potential upside and drawdown, and leveraged ETFs can decay quickly, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk. Increase QQQI to 25% for income generation. Sell QQQ and CRWD to reduce growth and high-beta exposure. Maintain a 10% cash buffer.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1%/month through monthly distributions, prioritizing low drawdown. While aiming for steady income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is not a savings account.
